node.html:
• Designed and owned the Noise Covariance block for ULPI-powered 32TR Massive MIMO radio unit, targeting high performance and area-efficient timing goals.
  • Knowledge on integrating high-speed Ethernet I/O interface IP, handling multi-clock domain designs with rigorous CDC debugging in simulation and hardware.
  • Contributed to micro-architecture and development of an Ethernet debug architecture, enabling a 25G SFP link for O-RAN Massive MIMO ULPI IP.
  • Debugged and optimized modem core IPs for the 5G NR UE PHY Baseband subsystem, specifically focusing on the Synchronization Signal Block (SSB) Receiver chain.
  • Collaborated with cross-functional teams across architecture, validation, and software to enable seamless HW-SW co-design and integrated new features into the SoC.
  • Improved productivity by exploring and implementing new automation tools to execute test plans and analyze results effectively.

• Assisted in developing fundamental DSP modules with fixed-point implementation in HDL, focusing on synthesis optimization and code linting.
  • Integrated Soft interface IPs such as DDR4 controllers and DMA to support required system functionality for 5G NR Systems.
  • Automated EDA tool flows by developing Make, Tcl, and Python scripts, significantly reducing manual effort.
  • Performed unit testing with Cocotb to ensure hardware reliability and functional correctness of RTL modules.
  • Managed build and automated testing environments by integrating Jenkins with Docker container agents.

• Gained foundational knowledge in digital design and communication systems by building basic modules with Vivado, Vitis, and MATLAB.
  • Designed and implemented basic DSP modules using High-Level Synthesis (HLS) for rapid prototyping of wireless communication blocks.
  • Developed digital logic for embedded systems using PlatformIO, Arduino, and IDF frameworks across Verilog, Assembly, and Avr-gcc.
  • Configured FPGA and ARM cores on the Vaman board using Termux for mobile-based hardware interaction and debugging.
  • Applied Matrix Analysis and optimization techniques (including cvxpy, gradient ascent, and descent) in Python to solve signal processing problems.
